content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807
. 428 J Street
. Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 . Fax 916) 322-0886
January26, 1999
Travis PiUs
Executive Director
California Building Standards Commission
1130 K Street, Suite 101
Sacramento, California 95814
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. G-98-307
Dear Mr. Pitts:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding Sections 11146
- 11146.4.1
The Commission can only provide advice regarding a person’s duties under the Political Reform
Act (the “Act”).
2 (Section 83114.) Sections 11146
- 11146.4 are outside the Commission’s
jurisdiction. However, we have provided our interpretation of the relevant sections of the Act to
assist you in determining your commissioner’s duties pursuant to Sections 11146
- 11146.4.
QUESTION
Are the commissioners of the California Building Standards Commission required to
receive mandatory ethics training pursuant to Sections 11146
- 11146.4?
CONCLUSION
Your commissioners are members of a state agency, as defined in the Act, and file
statements of economic interests pursuant to the Act. Therefore, we interpret the ethics training
requirements to apply to them, if the commissioners hold a position enumerated in Section
11 146(b)(1).
All references are to the Government Code.
2 Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. G-98-307
Page 2
FACTS
The California Building Standards Commission (“CBSC”) is comprised often members,
appointed by the Governor, subject to Senate confirmation and subject to filing requirements of
the Act. The authority for these appointments is contained in Health and Safety Code, division
13, part 2.5, chapter 2, article 1, commencing with Section 18920.
Last year AB 2179 was passed by the Legislature and signed by the Governor. (Ch. 364
(1998).) AB 2179 added Sections 11146 -11146.4, which are outside of the jurisdiction of the
Commission. This legislation requires all state agencies to conduct semi-annual orientation
courses that certain state officials and employees must attend once every two years. The
Commission, along with the Attorney General’s Office and the Department ofPersonnel
Administration, will be developing program content and materials.
ANALYSIS
Pursuant to Section 83114 ofthe Act, the Commission may provide written advice
regarding any person’s duties under the Act. The new mandatory ethics training program
instituted by Sections 11146
- 11146.4 is not part ofthe Act. Therefore, we cannot provide you
advice regarding your duties under the mandatory ethics training program.
The new legislation does cross reference certain definitions and provisions of the Act.
For example, the new legislation’s definition of a “state agency” incorporates the Act’s definition
of a “state agency.”
3 (Sections 11146 and 82049.) In addition, the new legislation’s definition of
“filer” includes persons required to file statements of economic interests under certain articles of
the Act. (Section 11146(b).) Therefore, to the extent that we can apply the relevant sections of
the Act to your commission and its members, we may be able to assist you in determining
whether your members are subject to the new legislation.
Under the Act, a state agency includes the CBSC. In addition, you state that the CBSC
members do file statements of economic interests pursuant to the Act. Therefore, at least one of
the two requirements ofthe term “filer,” as used in Section 11146(b), is met. The second
requirement is that the person hold a position listed in Section 11 146(b)(1). We conclude that
your commissioners are subject to the ethics training requirements if they satisfy Section
11 146(b)(l).
Under the Act, “state agency” means every state office, department, division, bureau, board and
conimission, and the Legislature. (Section 82049.)
Government Code Section 11146 does not include the
Legislature, however.


File No. G-98-307
Page 3
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Marte Castafios
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
SGC:MC:tls

requirements to apply to them, if the commissioners hold a position enumerated in Section
11 146(b)(1).
All references are to the Government Code.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Are the commissioners of the California Building Standards Commission required to
receive mandatory ethics training pursuant to Sections 11146
- 11146.4?

CONCLUSION: Your commissioners are members of a state agency, as defined in the Act, and file
statements of economic interests pursuant to the Act. Therefore, we interpret the ethics training
requirements to apply to them, if the commissioners hold a position enumerated in Section
11 146(b)(1).
All references are to the Government Code.
